question_text: Two electric heaters, X and Y, both raise the temperature of water by 10°C in different containers. Heater X has a power output of 1500 W and takes 8 minutes, whilst heater Y has a power output of 1200 W and takes 10 minutes. Which statement correctly compares the energy transferred by each heater?

options.label: A
options.text: Heater X transfers 720,000 J and heater Y transfers 720,000 J, so both transfer the same amount of energy

options.label: B
options.text: Heater X transfers 720,000 J and heater Y transfers 600,000 J, so heater X is more efficient

options.label: C
options.text: Heater Y transfers more energy because it takes longer to heat the water

options.label: D
options.text: Heater X transfers 12,000 J and heater Y transfers 12,000 J because power is constant

correct_answer: Heater X transfers 720,000 J and heater Y transfers 720,000 J, so both transfer the same amount of energy
explanation: To find energy transferred, use Energy = Power × Time. For heater X: E = 1500 W × 480 s (8 minutes converted to seconds) = 720,000 J. For heater Y: E = 1200 W × 600 s (10 minutes converted to seconds) = 720,000 J. Although heater X has higher power (faster rate), both transfer the same total energy because the lower-powered heater runs for longer. This shows that power and time are inversely related when energy is the same.
